headline: French hostages say they need more support after returning: 'Coming back was almost as hard as being detained. I felt isolated'
description: For many former French hostages and detainees held abroad, the ordeal continues upon their return to France, marked by a loss of bearings and a sense of abandonment.

headline: US hopes for progress, but Iran says no direct talks
The crowdfunding crisis calls its business model into question
Venezuela, Colombia pledge military cooperation on first post-Maduro visit
description: Delcy Rodriguez assumed power in Venezuela after a lightning US military raid on January 3 in Caracas, where socialist president Nicolas Maduro was captured and brought to New York to face drug trafficking charges. Colombian President Gustavo Petro has become the first foreign leader to visit Venezuela since Maduro's ousting.

headline: The Clio, understated queen of the French automotive landscape and top seller among business fleets
description: The best-selling French car across all generations, this compact city vehicle has been a fixture on the road for 35 years. Gradually transforming over time, it has carved out a prestigious place in corporate fleets.

headline: How tulips took over the world
description: Born in Central Asia, these bulb plants – whose name likely comes from their turban-like shape – have a long evolutionary history that is now better understood.

headline: How Tehran's propaganda lures the West into distraction
description: Pro-Iranian accounts are flooding the internet with AI-generated satirical videos that exploit Lego's popularity and Donald Trump's unpopularity, in an attempt to divert Western attention from the suffering of the Iranian people.

description: Freed from inhibition by the anonymity of the digital world, some gamers find liberation both for their avatars and themselves, leading them to coming out, transitioning or experimenting with their sexuality 'IRL.'

headline: 'Sudan, devastated by two warlords, faces the world's worst humanitarian crisis'
description: Le Monde columnist Alain Frachon reflects on more than three years of conflict in the African country of 50 million people.

headline: Historian Pascal Blanchard: 'Identity will be one of the main themes of France's presidential campaign'
description: The issue of identity, tackled head-on by both the far-right and the radical left, calls for political solutions to heal divisions as the 2027 presidential election approaches, explains Pascal Blanchard, an expert in colonization, in an interview.
